intent: Check whether the candidate can explain Express routing, HTTP methods, path parameters, query strings, and response status shaping in a simple REST endpoint.
primaryQuestion: How would you implement GET /users/:id in Express, read the path parameter and query options, and return the right HTTP response?
expectedSignals: Uses an HTTP method route such as app.get or router.get with /users/:id.
Reads route parameters from req.params and optional filters from req.query.
Distinguishes not found, validation failure, and successful responses with appropriate status codes.
Avoids returning raw internal data that should not be exposed.
redFlags: Confuses req.params, req.query, and req.body.
Always returns 200 even when the user is missing or the id is invalid.
Builds routes with manual string parsing instead of Express route parameters.

scoringHints.description: Walks through route declaration, parameter validation, data lookup, status code selection, and response shape with a concrete handler.
Covers routing and params but misses one detail such as response sanitization or invalid id handling.
Knows app.get but cannot explain request data sources or response status choices clearly.
Answer is too generic to validate practical Express routing knowledge.

followUps.question: Where exactly would userId and includePosts come from in req?
followUps.purpose: Verify route parameter versus query string understanding.

followUps.question: What would you return if the id format is invalid versus valid but not found?
followUps.purpose: Check API contract and HTTP status reasoning.

en.title: Express route parameters and responses
en.displayQuestion: How would you implement GET /users/:id in Express, read the path parameter and query options, and return the right HTTP response?
en.displayIntent: Check practical routing, request data access, and response shaping.
guidance: Use a concrete GET /users/:id example.
Explain params, query, validation, and status codes.
commonMistakes: Mixing up req.params and req.query.
Returning 200 for every outcome.

vi.title: Route parameter và response trong Express
vi.displayQuestion: Bạn sẽ implement GET /users/:id trong Express như thế nào để đọc path parameter, query option và trả về HTTP response đúng?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra khả năng dùng routing, đọc dữ liệu request và định hình response thực tế.
guidance: Dùng ví dụ cụ thể GET /users/:id.
Giải thích params, query, validation và status code.
commonMistakes: Nhầm req.params với req.query.
Luôn trả 200 cho mọi kết quả.

ja.title: Express の route parameter と response
ja.displayQuestion: Express で GET /users/:id を実装し、path parameter と query option を読み取り、適切な HTTP response を返すにはどうしますか。
ja.displayIntent: routing、request data の取得、response 設計の実務理解を確認する。
guidance: GET /users/:id の具体例を使う。
params、query、validation、status code を説明する。
commonMistakes: req.params と req.query を混同する。
すべての結果で 200 を返す。

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Routing
sourceReferences.note: Used for route methods, route paths, and route parameters.

sourceReferences.label: Express 4.x API: Request and Response
sourceReferences.note: Used for req.params, req.query, res.status, and res.json behavior.

intent: Evaluate whether the candidate understands Express middleware as an ordered request-response pipeline and knows when to call next or end the response.
primaryQuestion: In Express, what happens when a request passes through logging, JSON parsing, authentication, a route handler, and an error handler? Why does order matter?
expectedSignals: Explains that middleware runs in registration order for matching paths.
States that middleware should either send a response or call next.
Places body parsing before routes that need req.body and auth before protected routes.
Understands that error-handling middleware is separate and usually registered after routes.
redFlags: Believes middleware order is automatically optimized by Express.
Forgets next and leaves the request hanging.
Registers error handling or parsers in a position where they cannot affect the intended routes.

scoringHints.description: Accurately describes the full pipeline, short-circuit cases, next usage, and why registration order changes behavior.
Understands ordered middleware and next but misses one special case such as error handlers.
Can define middleware but cannot reason through a request flow.
Answer does not show practical middleware execution understanding.

followUps.question: What happens if auth middleware neither sends a response nor calls next?
Why would req.body be undefined if express.json is registered after the route?

en.title: Express middleware execution order
en.displayQuestion: In Express, what happens when a request passes through logging, JSON parsing, authentication, a route handler, and an error handler? Why does order matter?
en.displayIntent: Check middleware pipeline and next() fundamentals.
guidance: Walk through one request from app.use to route handler.
Mention when middleware stops or passes control.
commonMistakes: Forgetting next().
Registering middleware after routes that need it.

vi.title: Thứ tự chạy middleware Express
vi.displayQuestion: Trong Express, điều gì xảy ra khi request đi qua logging, JSON parser, authentication, route handler và error handler? Vì sao thứ tự quan trọng?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra nền tảng middleware pipeline và next().
guidance: Đi qua một request từ app.use tới route handler.
Nêu rõ khi nào middleware dừng hoặc chuyển quyền xử lý.
commonMistakes: Quên gọi next().
Đăng ký middleware sau route cần dùng nó.

ja.title: Express middleware の実行順序
ja.displayQuestion: Express で request が logging、JSON parsing、authentication、route handler、error handler を通るとき何が起きますか。なぜ順序が重要ですか。
ja.displayIntent: middleware pipeline と next() の基礎を確認する。
guidance: app.use から route handler まで 1 つの request を追う。
middleware が停止する場合と次へ渡す場合を説明する。
commonMistakes: next() を呼び忘れる。
必要な route の後に middleware を登録する。

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Using middleware
sourceReferences.note: Used for application-level middleware, router-level middleware, and middleware flow.

sourceReferences.label: EasyInterview: Express middleware interview guide
sourceReferences.note: Used for interview topic coverage; probe wording rewritten.

intent: Check whether the candidate understands built-in body parsers, content types, size limits, and why request body values must be treated as untrusted input.
primaryQuestion: When would you use express.json and express.urlencoded, what options would you configure, and why should req.body still be validated?
expectedSignals: Explains that express.json parses JSON payloads and express.urlencoded parses form-style payloads.
Mentions content type matching and middleware order before routes.
Configures limits to reduce accidental or malicious large body impact.
Treats req.body as untrusted and validates types, required fields, and allowed values.
redFlags: Assumes req.body is always trustworthy because Express parsed it.
Accepts unlimited payloads for public endpoints.
Uses the wrong parser for the content type or stacks parsers without understanding the resulting body shape.

scoringHints.description: Connects parser choice, Content-Type, limit configuration, validation, and endpoint-specific body contracts.
Explains json and urlencoded usage but misses one security or operational detail.
Knows to call express.json but cannot reason about limits or validation.
Answer does not demonstrate safe body parsing knowledge.

followUps.question: What limit would you set for a login endpoint versus a file-upload endpoint?
Can req.body.foo.toString() fail even after express.json parsed the request?

en.title: Body parsing and untrusted input
en.displayQuestion: When would you use express.json and express.urlencoded, what options would you configure, and why should req.body still be validated?
en.displayIntent: Check safe request body parsing and validation judgment.
guidance: Discuss parser type, Content-Type, middleware order, and limits.
Tie validation to user-controlled input.
commonMistakes: Trusting parsed body data blindly.
Leaving public JSON limits too large or unspecified.

vi.title: Parse body và dữ liệu không đáng tin
vi.displayQuestion: Khi nào bạn dùng express.json và express.urlencoded, bạn sẽ cấu hình option nào, và vì sao req.body vẫn phải được validate?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra judgment về parse request body an toàn và validation.
guidance: Nói về loại parser, Content-Type, thứ tự middleware và giới hạn payload.
Liên hệ validation với dữ liệu do user kiểm soát.
commonMistakes: Tin tưởng body đã parse một cách mù quáng.
Để limit JSON public endpoint quá lớn hoặc không rõ ràng.

ja.title: body parsing と信頼できない入力
ja.displayQuestion: express.json と express.urlencoded はいつ使い、どの option を設定し、なぜ req.body をさらに validate する必要がありますか。
ja.displayIntent: 安全な request body parsing と validation 判断を確認する。
guidance: parser type、Content-Type、middleware order、limit を説明する。
validation を user-controlled input と結びつける。
commonMistakes: parse 済み body をそのまま信頼する。
public endpoint の JSON limit を大きすぎるままにする。

sourceReferences.label: Express 4.x API: express.json and express.urlencoded
sourceReferences.note: Used for parser behavior, Content-Type matching, limit options, and untrusted req.body warning.

intent: Assess practical debugging of response lifecycle bugs such as sending multiple responses or calling next after a response has already been sent.
primaryQuestion: An Express route sometimes throws 'Cannot set headers after they are sent to the client'. How would you debug and fix it?
expectedSignals: Identifies multiple response paths as the likely cause.
Checks missing return statements after res.status(...).json(...) or res.redirect(...).
Mentions async branches that may both try to respond.
Uses res.headersSent carefully in error handlers and avoids continuing work after final response.
redFlags: Tries to suppress the error without finding the second response.
Calls next after already sending a normal response.
Adds arbitrary timeouts or global flags instead of fixing control flow.

scoringHints.description: Explains response lifecycle, traces all branches, fixes control flow, and handles late async errors safely.
Understands duplicate send bugs but misses one async or error-handler nuance.
Recognizes headers are already sent but cannot locate or prevent the second response.
Answer is generic debugging without Express-specific lifecycle reasoning.

followUps.question: Show a small route where a missing return causes this bug.
What should an error handler do if res.headersSent is already true?

en.title: Debugging duplicate Express responses
en.displayQuestion: An Express route sometimes throws 'Cannot set headers after they are sent to the client'. How would you debug and fix it?
en.displayIntent: Check response lifecycle debugging and control-flow discipline.
guidance: Look for multiple response branches.
Discuss missing returns, next usage, and late async failures.
commonMistakes: Ignoring the second response path.
Calling next after sending the final response.

vi.title: Debug duplicate response trong Express
vi.displayQuestion: Một Express route thỉnh thoảng báo 'Cannot set headers after they are sent to the client'. Bạn sẽ debug và sửa thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra khả năng debug vòng đời response và kiểm soát control flow.
guidance: Tìm các nhánh gửi response nhiều lần.
Nói về missing return, next usage và lỗi async xảy ra muộn.
commonMistakes: Bỏ qua nhánh gửi response thứ hai.
Gọi next sau khi đã gửi final response.

ja.title: Express の重複 response を debug する
ja.displayQuestion: Express route が時々 'Cannot set headers after they are sent to the client' を出します。どう debug して修正しますか。
ja.displayIntent: response lifecycle と control flow の debug 能力を確認する。
guidance: 複数の response branch を探す。
missing return、next usage、遅い async failure を説明する。
commonMistakes: 2 回目の response path を無視する。
final response 後に next を呼ぶ。

sourceReferences.label: Express 4.x API: res.headersSent
sourceReferences.note: Used for response lifecycle and headersSent behavior.

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Error handling
sourceReferences.note: Used for delegating to default error handler after headers are sent.

intent: Evaluate whether the candidate can organize Express routes using express.Router, mount paths, route-level middleware, and shared parameters.
primaryQuestion: How would you split user and order APIs into Express routers, mount them under /api, and apply middleware only to protected routes?
expectedSignals: Creates separate express.Router instances for feature route groups.
Mounts routers with app.use('/api/users', usersRouter) or similar.
Applies auth middleware at router or route level instead of globally when not needed.
Understands req.baseUrl, req.originalUrl, and mergeParams when nested routers need parent params.
redFlags: Keeps all routes in one large file without explaining boundaries.
Applies authentication globally and breaks public health or login routes.
Does not understand how mounted paths combine with router paths.

scoringHints.description: Shows clean router composition, mount paths, protected route middleware, and nested parameter trade-offs.
Understands router modules and mounting but misses nested params or route-level middleware nuance.
Can create routes but cannot describe maintainable router organization.
Answer does not validate practical Express app structure knowledge.

followUps.question: If orders are nested under /users/:userId/orders, how would the orders router access userId?
When would you mount auth middleware on a whole router versus a single route?

en.title: Express router modularization
en.displayQuestion: How would you split user and order APIs into Express routers, mount them under /api, and apply middleware only to protected routes?
en.displayIntent: Check route organization with express.Router and scoped middleware.
guidance: Use usersRouter and ordersRouter examples.
Explain mount paths and where auth middleware belongs.
commonMistakes: Putting every route in app.ts.
Making all endpoints protected accidentally.

vi.title: Tách module router trong Express
vi.displayQuestion: Bạn sẽ tách API user và order thành các Express router, mount dưới /api và chỉ áp dụng middleware cho route cần bảo vệ như thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra cách tổ chức route bằng express.Router và middleware theo scope.
guidance: Dùng ví dụ usersRouter và ordersRouter.
Giải thích mount path và vị trí đặt auth middleware.
commonMistakes: Nhét mọi route vào app.ts.
Vô tình bắt mọi endpoint đều phải auth.

ja.title: Express router の module 分割
ja.displayQuestion: user API と order API を Express router に分け、/api 配下に mount し、保護が必要な route だけ middleware を適用するにはどうしますか。
ja.displayIntent: express.Router と scoped middleware による route organization を確認する。
guidance: usersRouter と ordersRouter の例を使う。
mount path と auth middleware の位置を説明する。
commonMistakes: すべての route を app.ts に置く。
すべての endpoint を誤って protected にする。

sourceReferences.label: Express 4.x API: express.Router
sourceReferences.note: Used for Router creation, mount behavior, and mergeParams option.

sourceReferences.label: Simplilearn: Express.js interview questions
sourceReferences.note: Used for common interview coverage around route files; wording rewritten.

intent: Assess whether the candidate can design centralized Express error handling for synchronous errors, rejected promises, expected application errors, and safe response formatting.
primaryQuestion: How would you design centralized error handling in an Express API so validation errors, not-found errors, and unexpected failures are handled consistently?
expectedSignals: Uses a final error-handling middleware with the err, req, res, next signature.
Maps known application errors to stable status codes and response bodies.
Handles async route failures through try/catch with next(err) or Express 5 promise propagation.
Logs server-side details while avoiding leaking stack traces or secrets to clients.
redFlags: Wraps every response in 200 with an error field.
Leaks err.stack or database details in production responses.
Assumes Express 4 automatically handles all async promise rejections.

scoringHints.description: Covers centralized mapping, async propagation, logging, production-safe response bodies, and headers-sent fallback.
Understands error middleware and status mapping but misses Express 4 versus 5 async differences or logging details.
Mentions try/catch but cannot show how errors reach the centralized handler.
Answer does not prove reliable production error handling knowledge.

followUps.question: How does your answer change between Express 4 and Express 5 for an async route that throws?
Which error fields should never be returned to an external client?

en.title: Centralized Express error handling
en.displayQuestion: How would you design centralized error handling in an Express API so validation errors, not-found errors, and unexpected failures are handled consistently?
en.displayIntent: Check production-grade error propagation and response consistency.
guidance: Describe error middleware placement and signature.
Explain error classification, logging, and client-safe responses.
commonMistakes: Returning 200 with error payloads.
Leaking internal stack traces.

vi.title: Error handling tập trung trong Express
vi.displayQuestion: Bạn sẽ thiết kế error handling tập trung trong Express API thế nào để validation error, not-found error và unexpected failure được xử lý nhất quán?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra error propagation và response consistency ở mức production.
guidance: Mô tả vị trí và signature của error middleware.
Giải thích phân loại lỗi, logging và response an toàn cho client.
commonMistakes: Trả 200 kèm error payload.
Lộ stack trace nội bộ.

ja.title: Express の centralized error handling
ja.displayQuestion: Express API で validation error、not-found error、unexpected failure を一貫して処理する centralized error handling をどう設計しますか。
ja.displayIntent: production レベルの error propagation と response consistency を確認する。
guidance: error middleware の配置と signature を説明する。
error classification、logging、client-safe response を説明する。
commonMistakes: error payload を 200 で返す。
内部 stack trace を漏らす。

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Error handling
sourceReferences.note: Used for error middleware signature, next(err), and default error handler behavior.

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Moving to Express 5
sourceReferences.note: Used for rejected promise behavior in Express 5.

intent: Evaluate whether the candidate can build authentication and authorization middleware with clear request augmentation, error semantics, and route-level authorization.
primaryQuestion: How would you implement Express middleware that authenticates a bearer token, attaches the current user, and enforces role-based access on selected routes?
expectedSignals: Separates authentication from authorization checks.
Validates bearer token format, verifies the token, and handles expired or invalid credentials.
Attaches a minimal trusted user context to req for downstream handlers.
Uses 401 for unauthenticated and 403 for authenticated but unauthorized requests.
redFlags: Stores full sensitive token claims or secrets on req.
Uses 403 and 401 interchangeably without explaining semantics.
Checks roles in controllers inconsistently instead of using a reusable policy or middleware.

scoringHints.description: Explains token parsing, verification, request context, route-scoped authorization, status semantics, and testing strategy.
Covers auth middleware and roles but misses one detail such as context minimization or error semantics.
Can sketch token verification but mixes authentication and authorization responsibilities.
Answer lacks enough detail to trust protected endpoint behavior.

followUps.question: What exact fields would you put on req.user and which fields would you avoid?
When should authorization live in middleware versus inside the service layer?

en.title: Authentication and authorization middleware
en.displayQuestion: How would you implement Express middleware that authenticates a bearer token, attaches the current user, and enforces role-based access on selected routes?
en.displayIntent: Check protected route design and auth middleware boundaries.
guidance: Separate authentication from authorization.
Discuss req.user shape, 401 versus 403, and route scope.
commonMistakes: Mixing 401 and 403.
Leaking sensitive token data into downstream code.

vi.title: Middleware authentication và authorization
vi.displayQuestion: Bạn sẽ implement middleware Express để xác thực bearer token, gắn current user và enforce role-based access trên một số route như thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra thiết kế protected route và ranh giới auth middleware.
guidance: Tách authentication khỏi authorization.
Nói về shape của req.user, 401 so với 403 và scope route.
commonMistakes: Nhầm 401 với 403.
Đưa dữ liệu token nhạy cảm xuống downstream code.

ja.title: authentication と authorization middleware
ja.displayQuestion: bearer token を認証し、current user を付与し、選択した route で role-based access を強制する Express middleware をどう実装しますか。
ja.displayIntent: protected route 設計と auth middleware boundary を確認する。
guidance: authentication と authorization を分ける。
req.user の形、401 と 403、route scope を説明する。
commonMistakes: 401 と 403 を混同する。
sensitive token data を downstream code に渡す。

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Using middleware
sourceReferences.note: Used for route-level and router-level middleware placement.

sourceReferences.label: Express security best practices
sourceReferences.note: Used for production security framing around sensitive data and authorization endpoints.

intent: Assess whether the candidate can design request validation for Express APIs without mixing transport validation, business rules, and persistence constraints.
primaryQuestion: Where would you put validation in an Express API, and how would you distinguish request shape validation from business rule validation?
expectedSignals: Uses middleware or controller-adjacent validation for request shape, types, and required fields.
Keeps domain/business invariants in service or domain logic where they can be reused.
Returns consistent 400 or 422 style client errors with field-level details when appropriate.
Avoids trusting req.body, req.query, or req.params without validation and normalization.
redFlags: Validates only on the frontend.
Places every business rule in generic middleware and loses domain context.
Lets database constraint errors become the main user-facing validation experience.

scoringHints.description: Separates transport schemas from domain rules, handles normalization, response details, and reuse across HTTP and non-HTTP callers.
Understands middleware validation but misses one boundary such as service-level invariants.
Knows validation is needed but cannot place it cleanly in the architecture.
Answer does not show reliable API validation design.

followUps.question: Which validation rules should remain in the service even if you already validate with middleware?
How would you return validation errors for multiple invalid fields?

en.title: Validation boundaries in Express APIs
en.displayQuestion: Where would you put validation in an Express API, and how would you distinguish request shape validation from business rule validation?
en.displayIntent: Check validation placement and API boundary judgment.
guidance: Separate request schema validation from domain rules.
Mention error response structure and normalization.
commonMistakes: Relying only on frontend validation.
Treating database errors as the primary validation layer.

vi.title: Ranh giới validation trong Express API
vi.displayQuestion: Bạn sẽ đặt validation ở đâu trong Express API, và phân biệt validation shape của request với business rule như thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra judgment về vị trí validation và ranh giới API.
guidance: Tách request schema validation khỏi domain rule.
Nhắc tới cấu trúc error response và normalization.
commonMistakes: Chỉ dựa vào frontend validation.
Coi database error là lớp validation chính.

ja.title: Express API の validation boundary
ja.displayQuestion: Express API で validation をどこに置き、request shape validation と business rule validation をどう区別しますか。
ja.displayIntent: validation placement と API boundary の判断を確認する。
guidance: request schema validation と domain rule を分ける。
error response structure と normalization に触れる。
commonMistakes: frontend validation だけに頼る。
database error を主要な validation layer とする。

sourceReferences.label: Express 4.x API: req.body warning
sourceReferences.note: Used for the warning that request body values are user-controlled and untrusted.

sourceReferences.label: Express security best practices: Do not trust user input
sourceReferences.note: Used for input validation and handling guidance.

intent: Check whether the candidate can safely serve static assets with express.static, caching options, dotfile behavior, fallback behavior, and reverse proxy considerations.
primaryQuestion: If an Express app must serve uploaded public assets and a SPA build, how would you configure express.static safely and efficiently?
expectedSignals: Mounts static middleware to an explicit public URL path and filesystem root.
Configures caching such as maxAge or immutable only for versioned assets.
Understands fallthrough behavior and custom 404 handling.
Considers dotfiles, directory traversal boundaries, and reverse proxy caching.
redFlags: Serves the project root or uploads directory without considering private files.
Enables long cache headers for non-versioned files that may change.
Relies on Express alone for high-volume static asset performance.

scoringHints.description: Covers mount paths, filesystem root safety, cache strategy, dotfiles, fallthrough, and proxy/CDN offload.
Knows express.static and caching but misses one safety or production detail.
Can serve files but cannot reason about cache or exposure risks.
Answer lacks enough detail for safe static asset serving.

followUps.question: Which files can safely use immutable long-term caching and which cannot?
What can go wrong if you serve the repository root as static content?

en.title: Serving static assets safely
en.displayQuestion: If an Express app must serve uploaded public assets and a SPA build, how would you configure express.static safely and efficiently?
en.displayIntent: Check static file serving, caching, and exposure-risk reasoning.
guidance: Discuss mount path, root directory, cache headers, and fallthrough.
Mention reverse proxy or CDN when relevant.
commonMistakes: Serving too broad a filesystem directory.
Caching mutable files for too long.

vi.title: Serve static asset an toàn
vi.displayQuestion: Nếu một Express app phải serve public uploaded assets và SPA build, bạn sẽ cấu hình express.static thế nào để vừa an toàn vừa hiệu quả?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra hiểu biết về static file serving, caching và rủi ro lộ file.
guidance: Nói về mount path, root directory, cache header và fallthrough.
Nhắc tới reverse proxy hoặc CDN khi phù hợp.
commonMistakes: Serve một thư mục filesystem quá rộng.
Cache file có thể thay đổi quá lâu.

ja.title: static asset を安全に配信する
ja.displayQuestion: Express app が public uploaded assets と SPA build を配信する必要がある場合、express.static を安全かつ効率的にどう設定しますか。
ja.displayIntent: static file serving、caching、file exposure risk の理解を確認する。
guidance: mount path、root directory、cache header、fallthrough を説明する。
必要に応じて reverse proxy や CDN に触れる。
commonMistakes: 広すぎる filesystem directory を配信する。
変更される file を長期間 cache する。

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Serving static files
sourceReferences.note: Used for express.static setup and static directory mounting.

sourceReferences.label: Express 4.x API: express.static
sourceReferences.note: Used for cache, dotfiles, fallthrough, and static middleware options.

intent: Assess whether the candidate understands practical Express production security controls including Helmet, TLS assumptions, fingerprint reduction, user input validation, and dependency hygiene.
primaryQuestion: What security middleware and production settings would you add to a public Express API before launch, and what risk does each one reduce?
expectedSignals: Uses Helmet or equivalent response security headers and can name examples such as CSP, HSTS, frame protection, or MIME sniffing prevention.
Disables or reduces obvious fingerprinting such as X-Powered-By.
Validates and sanitizes user input instead of trusting request data.
Mentions TLS termination, secure dependencies, and endpoint-specific brute-force/rate-limit protection.
redFlags: Says installing Helmet makes the app secure by itself.
Ignores TLS, cookies, dependency vulnerabilities, or input validation.
Returns verbose production errors that reveal internals.

scoringHints.description: Connects concrete Express controls to specific risks and distinguishes framework-level hardening from app-specific validation and authorization.
Lists useful controls but cannot explain one or two risks clearly.
Knows a few package names but lacks production security reasoning.
Answer is too shallow to validate security readiness.

followUps.question: Which Helmet defaults would you review for a JSON API versus a browser-rendered app?
Why is disabling X-Powered-By useful but not sufficient security?

en.title: Express production security hardening
en.displayQuestion: What security middleware and production settings would you add to a public Express API before launch, and what risk does each one reduce?
en.displayIntent: Check practical security hardening for Express APIs.
guidance: Name specific controls and risks.
Separate framework headers from app-specific validation and authorization.
commonMistakes: Treating Helmet as complete security.
Ignoring input validation and dependency hygiene.

vi.title: Hardening bảo mật Express production
vi.displayQuestion: Trước khi launch một public Express API, bạn sẽ thêm security middleware và production setting nào, và mỗi thứ giảm rủi ro gì?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra khả năng hardening bảo mật thực tế cho Express API.
guidance: Nêu control cụ thể và rủi ro tương ứng.
Tách framework header khỏi validation và authorization của app.
commonMistakes: Coi Helmet là đủ để bảo mật toàn bộ.
Bỏ qua input validation và dependency hygiene.

ja.title: Express production security hardening
ja.displayQuestion: public Express API を launch する前にどの security middleware と production setting を追加し、それぞれどの risk を下げますか。
ja.displayIntent: Express API の実務的な security hardening を確認する。
guidance: 具体的な control と risk を挙げる。
framework header と app 固有の validation/authorization を分ける。
commonMistakes: Helmet だけで十分と考える。
input validation と dependency hygiene を無視する。

sourceReferences.label: Express security best practices

intent: Evaluate whether the candidate can reason about cookie security, session storage choices, proxy/TLS interactions, and what data should never live in client-visible session cookies.
primaryQuestion: How would you configure cookies and sessions in an Express app behind HTTPS, and how would you choose between server-side sessions and cookie-backed sessions?
expectedSignals: Sets secure, httpOnly, sameSite, path/domain, and expiry settings according to the use case.
Understands secure cookies require HTTPS and often correct trust proxy settings behind a proxy.
Distinguishes server-side session IDs from cookie-backed session payloads.
Avoids storing secrets or large sensitive data in client-visible cookies.
redFlags: Stores passwords, access tokens, or large profile data directly in a cookie-backed session.
Uses the default in-memory session store in production.
Enables secure cookies behind a proxy without understanding why they may not be set.

scoringHints.description: Explains cookie flags, trust proxy/TLS, storage model trade-offs, production session store needs, and sensitive data boundaries.
Covers cookie flags and basic session choice but misses proxy or production store details.
Knows sessions exist but cannot reason about security properties.
Answer is too generic for secure session design.

followUps.question: What changes when the session is stored server-side versus serialized into the cookie?
Why might a secure cookie fail locally or behind a load balancer?

en.title: Secure cookies and session storage
en.displayQuestion: How would you configure cookies and sessions in an Express app behind HTTPS, and how would you choose between server-side sessions and cookie-backed sessions?
en.displayIntent: Check cookie security and session storage trade-offs.
guidance: Discuss cookie flags and trust proxy.
Compare server-side session store with cookie-backed storage.
commonMistakes: Using memory session store in production.
Putting sensitive payloads in client-visible cookies.

vi.title: Cookie an toàn và session storage
vi.displayQuestion: Bạn sẽ cấu hình cookie và session trong Express app chạy sau HTTPS như thế nào, và chọn server-side session hay cookie-backed session dựa trên tiêu chí gì?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra bảo mật cookie và trade-off khi lưu session.
guidance: Nói về cookie flag và trust proxy.
So sánh server-side session store với cookie-backed storage.
commonMistakes: Dùng memory session store ở production.
Đưa payload nhạy cảm vào cookie client có thể thấy.

ja.title: secure cookie と session storage
ja.displayQuestion: HTTPS 配下の Express app で cookie と session をどう設定し、server-side session と cookie-backed session をどう選びますか。
ja.displayIntent: cookie security と session storage trade-off を確認する。
guidance: cookie flags と trust proxy を説明する。
server-side session store と cookie-backed storage を比較する。
commonMistakes: production で memory session store を使う。
client-visible cookie に sensitive payload を入れる。

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Behind proxies

intent: Check whether the candidate understands Express trust proxy behavior and how it affects req.ip, req.protocol, secure cookies, logging, and rate limiting.
primaryQuestion: Your Express API runs behind Nginx or a cloud load balancer. How would you configure trust proxy, and what breaks if it is wrong?
expectedSignals: Explains that trust proxy controls whether Express trusts X-Forwarded-* headers from upstream proxies.
Understands impact on req.ip, req.ips, req.protocol, req.hostname, secure cookies, redirects, logs, and rate limits.
Chooses a constrained trust proxy setting based on known proxy hops or IP ranges instead of blindly trusting all clients.
Verifies that the last trusted proxy strips or overwrites spoofable forwarded headers.
redFlags: Sets trust proxy to true everywhere without understanding spoofing risk.
Builds rate limiting on req.ip while trusting client-controlled X-Forwarded-For.
Ignores secure cookie behavior behind TLS termination.

scoringHints.description: Connects trust proxy settings to forwarded headers, spoofing risk, secure cookies, redirects, logs, and rate-limit correctness.
Understands why trust proxy is needed but misses one downstream behavior.
Knows there is a setting but cannot reason about security or operational impact.
Answer does not validate proxy deployment knowledge.

followUps.question: How would req.ip be derived differently when trust proxy is disabled versus enabled?
Why can trusting X-Forwarded-For from the open internet break rate limiting?

en.title: Express behind a trusted proxy
en.displayQuestion: Your Express API runs behind Nginx or a cloud load balancer. How would you configure trust proxy, and what breaks if it is wrong?
en.displayIntent: Check proxy-aware Express deployment knowledge.
guidance: Discuss forwarded headers and trusted hops.
Tie the setting to IP logging, rate limits, protocol, and secure cookies.
commonMistakes: Trusting all forwarded headers blindly.
Building security controls on spoofable client IPs.

vi.title: Express chạy sau trusted proxy
vi.displayQuestion: Express API của bạn chạy sau Nginx hoặc cloud load balancer. Bạn sẽ cấu hình trust proxy thế nào, và cấu hình sai sẽ làm hỏng điều gì?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra hiểu biết triển khai Express có proxy.
guidance: Nói về forwarded headers và trusted hops.
Liên hệ setting với IP logging, rate limit, protocol và secure cookie.
commonMistakes: Tin toàn bộ forwarded header một cách mù quáng.
Dựng security control trên client IP có thể bị spoof.

ja.title: trusted proxy 配下の Express
ja.displayQuestion: Express API が Nginx または cloud load balancer の後ろで動いています。trust proxy をどう設定し、間違えると何が壊れますか。
ja.displayIntent: proxy-aware な Express deployment の理解を確認する。
guidance: forwarded headers と trusted hops を説明する。
IP logging、rate limit、protocol、secure cookie と結びつける。
commonMistakes: すべての forwarded header を無条件に信頼する。
spoof 可能な client IP に security control を置く。

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Behind proxies
sourceReferences.note: Used for trust proxy modes and X-Forwarded-* behavior.

intent: Assess debugging ability for async Express route failures, swallowed promise rejections, missing awaits, and inconsistent error responses.
primaryQuestion: A production Express endpoint sometimes times out and logs an unhandled promise rejection from inside an async database call. How would you investigate and fix it?
expectedSignals: Checks whether the route awaits promises and forwards failures to the error handler.
Distinguishes Express 4 async wrapper or try/catch needs from Express 5 rejected promise handling.
Looks for branches that neither send a response nor call next.
Adds tests for success, expected failure, and rejected promise paths.
redFlags: Adds a global unhandledRejection handler as the only fix.
Catches errors and does nothing, causing the request to hang.
Mixes callback and promise styles without ensuring exactly one completion path.

scoringHints.description: Traces promise lifecycle, Express version behavior, response completion paths, centralized error forwarding, and regression tests.
Understands try/catch and next(err) but misses Express 5 or testing details.
Can identify async error but cannot fix control flow reliably.
Answer does not show enough Express async debugging skill.

followUps.question: What is the smallest route example where Express 4 fails to catch a rejected promise without help?
What logs or traces would confirm the request is hanging rather than returning a slow 500?

en.title: Async route rejection debugging
en.displayQuestion: A production Express endpoint sometimes times out and logs an unhandled promise rejection from inside an async database call. How would you investigate and fix it?

guidance: Discuss awaits, try/catch, next(err), and Express version.
Mention tests for rejected promise paths.
commonMistakes: Only adding a global unhandledRejection handler.
Catching errors without responding or forwarding.

vi.title: Debug rejected promise trong async route
vi.displayQuestion: Một Express endpoint production thỉnh thoảng timeout và log unhandled promise rejection bên trong async database call. Bạn sẽ điều tra và sửa thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra khả năng debug async route error và completion path.
guidance: Nói về await, try/catch, next(err) và version Express.
Nhắc tới test cho rejected promise path.
commonMistakes: Chỉ thêm global unhandledRejection handler.
Catch lỗi nhưng không response hoặc forward.

ja.title: async route rejection の debug
ja.displayQuestion: production の Express endpoint が時々 timeout し、async database call 内の unhandled promise rejection を log します。どう調査して修正しますか。
ja.displayIntent: async route error debugging と completion path reasoning を確認する。
guidance: await、try/catch、next(err)、Express version を説明する。
rejected promise path の test に触れる。
commonMistakes: global unhandledRejection handler だけを追加する。

intent: Evaluate whether the candidate can configure CORS intentionally for Express APIs, explain preflight behavior, and avoid over-permissive credentials policies.
primaryQuestion: How would you configure CORS for an Express API used by a browser frontend, especially when cookies or Authorization headers are involved?
expectedSignals: Explains origins, methods, allowed headers, credentials, and preflight requests.
Avoids combining wildcard origin with credentialed browser requests.
Uses an allowlist or environment-specific origin policy rather than fully open production CORS.
Separates CORS from authentication and server-to-server authorization concerns.
redFlags: Sets Access-Control-Allow-Origin to * and credentials to true for production.
Treats CORS as a complete security control for APIs.
Cannot explain why OPTIONS preflight happens.

scoringHints.description: Explains browser CORS mechanics, preflight, credentialed requests, allowlists, and how CORS differs from auth.
Understands origin allowlists and credentials but misses one preflight or header detail.
Knows to install cors middleware but cannot explain browser behavior.
Answer is too shallow for production CORS configuration.

followUps.question: Why does adding an Authorization header often trigger an OPTIONS request first?
Why is wildcard origin dangerous or invalid when credentials are required?

en.title: CORS for Express APIs
en.displayQuestion: How would you configure CORS for an Express API used by a browser frontend, especially when cookies or Authorization headers are involved?
en.displayIntent: Check browser CORS mechanics and safe production policy design.
guidance: Discuss origin allowlists, preflight, headers, methods, and credentials.
Clarify that CORS is not authentication.
commonMistakes: Using wildcard origins with credentials.
Confusing CORS with API authorization.

vi.title: CORS cho Express API
vi.displayQuestion: Bạn sẽ cấu hình CORS cho Express API được browser frontend gọi như thế nào, đặc biệt khi có cookie hoặc Authorization header?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra cơ chế CORS của browser và thiết kế policy production an toàn.
guidance: Nói về origin allowlist, preflight, header, method và credentials.
Làm rõ CORS không phải authentication.
commonMistakes: Dùng wildcard origin với credentials.
Nhầm CORS với authorization của API.

ja.title: Express API の CORS

ja.displayIntent: browser CORS mechanics と安全な production policy design を確認する。
guidance: origin allowlist、preflight、headers、methods、credentials を説明する。
CORS は authentication ではないと明確にする。
commonMistakes: credentials と wildcard origin を組み合わせる。
CORS と API authorization を混同する。

intent: Assess whether the candidate can test Express routes by separating app creation from server listening, mocking dependencies, and covering middleware/error paths.
primaryQuestion: How would you structure an Express app so routes can be integration-tested without opening a real network port?
expectedSignals: Separates app construction from app.listen so tests can import the app instance.
Uses HTTP-level tests against the Express app with controlled dependencies or a test database.
Covers middleware behavior, validation errors, auth failures, success responses, and error middleware.
Avoids brittle tests that assert implementation details instead of API contracts.
redFlags: Starts the production server as a side effect of importing app code.
Only unit-tests controller functions and never tests middleware composition.
Depends on shared mutable production data during tests.

scoringHints.description: Explains app/server separation, dependency injection or test doubles, HTTP contract tests, error-path coverage, and deterministic cleanup.
Understands importing app for tests but misses one dependency or cleanup detail.
Can test individual functions but not Express route composition.
Answer does not validate practical API test design.

followUps.question: What file would export app, and what file would call app.listen?
Which route behaviors would you require before considering the endpoint covered?

en.title: Testing Express route composition

guidance: Discuss app export versus server listen.
Mention tests for middleware, success, validation, auth, and error paths.
commonMistakes: Calling app.listen during import.
Testing only controller internals.

vi.title: Test composition route Express
vi.displayQuestion: Bạn sẽ tổ chức Express app thế nào để có thể integration test route mà không cần mở network port thật?

guidance: Nói về export app so với server listen.

commonMistakes: Gọi app.listen ngay khi import.
Chỉ test nội bộ controller.

ja.title: Express route composition の test

intent: Evaluate whether the candidate can reason about Express production performance across code, runtime, and infrastructure layers.
primaryQuestion: An Express API has poor p95 latency under load. How would you investigate and improve performance without guessing?
expectedSignals: Starts with measurement: p50/p95/p99 latency, throughput, error rate, CPU, memory, event loop delay, database timing, and downstream timing.
Looks for synchronous work, blocking I/O, excessive JSON parsing/stringifying, inefficient logging, and slow middleware.
Considers compression, caching, reverse proxy, load balancing, clustering, and NODE_ENV=production.
Validates changes with load tests and production telemetry rather than micro-optimizing blindly.
redFlags: Immediately adds more servers without identifying the bottleneck.
Uses synchronous APIs in request paths and ignores event loop blocking.
Enables compression or logging changes without measuring CPU and latency trade-offs.

scoringHints.description: Presents a measurement-led plan across Express middleware, Node runtime, database/downstream dependencies, caching, compression, process model, and verification.
Covers common Express performance practices but misses one layer such as downstream tracing or validation.
Lists optimizations without a diagnostic method.
Answer does not show production performance engineering skill.

followUps.question: Which metric would tell you the event loop is blocked rather than the database being slow?
When can gzip compression make latency worse?
followUps.purpose: Check CPU versus bandwidth trade-off reasoning.

en.title: Express performance investigation
en.displayQuestion: An Express API has poor p95 latency under load. How would you investigate and improve performance without guessing?
en.displayIntent: Check production performance diagnosis and improvement strategy.
guidance: Start from metrics and traces.
Cover code, middleware, runtime, database, cache, and infrastructure layers.
commonMistakes: Scaling before identifying the bottleneck.
Ignoring synchronous request-path work.

vi.title: Điều tra performance Express
vi.displayQuestion: Một Express API có p95 latency kém khi chịu tải. Bạn sẽ điều tra và cải thiện như thế nào mà không đoán mò?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra chiến lược chẩn đoán và cải thiện performance production.
guidance: Bắt đầu từ metric và trace.
Bao phủ code, middleware, runtime, database, cache và infrastructure.
commonMistakes: Scale trước khi biết bottleneck.
Bỏ qua synchronous work trong request path.

ja.title: Express performance investigation
ja.displayQuestion: Express API が load 下で p95 latency が悪いです。推測ではなく、どう調査して改善しますか。
ja.displayIntent: production performance diagnosis と improvement strategy を確認する。
guidance: metrics と traces から始める。
code、middleware、runtime、database、cache、infrastructure を扱う。
commonMistakes: bottleneck 特定前に scale する。
request path の synchronous work を無視する。

sourceReferences.label: Express performance best practices
sourceReferences.note: Used for compression, avoiding synchronous functions, logging, NODE_ENV, clustering, caching, load balancers, and reverse proxy guidance.

intent: Assess whether the candidate can design health checks and graceful shutdown for Express services in production deployments.
primaryQuestion: How would you implement health checks and graceful shutdown for an Express service running in containers or behind a load balancer?
expectedSignals: Separates liveness from readiness and includes dependency health where appropriate.
On shutdown signal, stops accepting new connections and allows in-flight requests to finish within a deadline.
Closes database, message queue, and other resource pools cleanly.
Coordinates with load balancer or orchestrator readiness so traffic drains before process exit.
redFlags: Kills the process immediately on SIGTERM without draining requests.
Health endpoint always returns 200 even when critical dependencies are unavailable.
Keeps accepting new traffic while resources are being closed.

scoringHints.description: Covers readiness/liveness semantics, signal handling, server.close, inflight deadlines, dependency cleanup, traffic draining, and observability.
Understands graceful shutdown basics but misses one orchestration or dependency detail.
Can add /health but lacks lifecycle management.
Answer is not sufficient for production deployment safety.

followUps.question: What should readiness return while the app is draining during shutdown?
What would you monitor to know shutdown is causing dropped requests?

en.title: Health checks and graceful shutdown
en.displayQuestion: How would you implement health checks and graceful shutdown for an Express service running in containers or behind a load balancer?
en.displayIntent: Check production lifecycle management for Express services.
guidance: Separate liveness, readiness, and dependency health.
Discuss SIGTERM, server.close, request draining, deadlines, and resource cleanup.
commonMistakes: Exiting immediately on SIGTERM.
Returning healthy while critical dependencies are down.

vi.title: Health check và graceful shutdown
vi.displayQuestion: Bạn sẽ implement health check và graceful shutdown cho Express service chạy trong container hoặc sau load balancer như thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra quản lý lifecycle production cho Express service.
guidance: Tách liveness, readiness và dependency health.
Nói về SIGTERM, server.close, drain request, deadline và cleanup resource.
commonMistakes: Thoát process ngay khi nhận SIGTERM.
Trả healthy khi dependency critical đang down.

ja.title: health check と graceful shutdown
ja.displayQuestion: container または load balancer 配下の Express service で health checks と graceful shutdown をどう実装しますか。
ja.displayIntent: Express service の production lifecycle management を確認する。
guidance: liveness、readiness、dependency health を分ける。
SIGTERM、server.close、request draining、deadline、resource cleanup を説明する。
commonMistakes: SIGTERM で即 exit する。
critical dependency が down でも healthy を返す。

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Health checks and graceful shutdown
sourceReferences.note: Used for health check and graceful shutdown production patterns.

intent: Evaluate whether the candidate can structure a larger Express service with clear boundaries between routing, controllers, services, repositories, policies, and cross-cutting middleware.
primaryQuestion: For a growing Express codebase, how would you structure routes, controllers, services, repositories, middleware, and configuration so the app stays maintainable?
expectedSignals: Keeps Express-specific concerns near routing/controllers and domain logic in services or domain modules.
Uses explicit dependency boundaries for databases, external clients, configuration, and policies.
Avoids turning middleware into a dumping ground for business logic.
Designs for testability, consistent errors, observability, and gradual feature ownership.
redFlags: Puts all business logic in route handlers.
Introduces heavy abstractions without a concrete maintenance problem.
Uses global mutable singletons everywhere and makes tests order-dependent.

scoringHints.description: Balances pragmatic Express simplicity with clear module boundaries, dependency management, testability, observability, and team ownership.
Has a reasonable layered structure but misses one cross-cutting concern or dependency boundary.
Suggests folders but cannot explain responsibilities and trade-offs.

vi.displayQuestion: Với một Express codebase đang lớn dần, bạn sẽ tổ chức routes, controllers, services, repositories, middleware và configuration thế nào để app dễ maintain?

intent: Assess whether the candidate can plan an Express 4 to Express 5 migration with attention to route matching, removed APIs, async error behavior, body parsing defaults, and regression testing.
primaryQuestion: What would you check when migrating an Express 4 service to Express 5, and how would you reduce the risk of breaking routes or error handling?
expectedSignals: Runs automated tests and reviews route matching syntax changes such as named wildcards and optional segments.
Removes deprecated response signatures like res.send(body, status) and res.json(obj, status).
Understands rejected promises from async handlers are forwarded in Express 5.
Checks express.urlencoded defaults, req.body behavior, static dotfile changes, and app.listen error callback behavior.
redFlags: Assumes Express 5 is a drop-in upgrade without reading breaking changes.
Migrates route patterns without regression tests for important URLs.
Keeps deprecated response signatures and only tests happy paths.

scoringHints.description: Covers breaking API removals, path syntax, async errors, parser/static defaults, codemods, compatibility tests, canary rollout, and rollback.
Understands main migration risks but misses one specific change or rollout detail.
Knows there is a new version but cannot identify concrete breaking changes.
Answer is not sufficient to trust a production migration.

Discuss rollout and rollback controls.

intent: Evaluate senior-level incident response for Express APIs, including triage, rollback, observability, customer impact, and durable fixes.
primaryQuestion: A new Express release causes a spike in 500s and login latency. How would you triage, mitigate, and follow up?
expectedSignals: Checks error rate, latency percentiles, affected routes, deploy timing, logs, traces, and dependency health.
Mitigates with rollback, feature flag disablement, traffic shift, or rate limiting depending on blast radius.
Looks for common Express-specific causes such as auth middleware order, body parser limits, session store issues, trust proxy changes, or async error handling regressions.
Communicates impact, owns a post-incident review, adds tests/alerts, and validates the durable fix.
redFlags: Keeps debugging in production while impact grows and no mitigation is attempted.
Blames infrastructure without correlating with deploys or route-level telemetry.
Closes the incident after rollback without a follow-up fix or regression test.

scoringHints.description: Balances rapid mitigation with evidence-based diagnosis, Express-specific hypotheses, stakeholder communication, and durable prevention.
Has a reasonable incident flow but misses one dimension such as communication or permanent fix validation.
Can debug technically but does not manage customer impact or follow-up quality.
Answer does not show senior production ownership.

followUps.question: Which dashboard panels would you open in the first five minutes?
What Express-specific regressions would you check first for login latency?

en.title: Express production incident response
en.displayQuestion: A new Express release causes a spike in 500s and login latency. How would you triage, mitigate, and follow up?
en.displayIntent: Check senior incident response and Express-specific production reasoning.
guidance: Start with impact, telemetry, and deploy correlation.
Discuss mitigation, root cause, communication, and durable prevention.
commonMistakes: Debugging too long before mitigation.
Rolling back without a follow-up fix or test.

vi.title: Ứng phó incident Express production
vi.displayQuestion: Một release Express mới làm tăng 500s và latency login. Bạn sẽ triage, mitigate và follow up như thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra incident response senior và reasoning production theo Express.
guidance: Bắt đầu từ impact, telemetry và tương quan với deploy.
Nói về mitigation, root cause, communication và phòng ngừa lâu dài.
commonMistakes: Debug quá lâu trước khi mitigate.
Rollback xong nhưng không có fix hoặc test follow-up.

ja.title: Express production incident response
ja.displayQuestion: 新しい Express release により 500s と login latency が急増しました。どう triage、mitigate、follow up しますか。
ja.displayIntent: senior incident response と Express-specific production reasoning を確認する。
guidance: impact、telemetry、deploy correlation から始める。
mitigation、root cause、communication、durable prevention を説明する。
commonMistakes: mitigation 前に長く debug しすぎる。
rollback 後に follow-up fix や test を追加しない。

sourceReferences.note: Used for production reliability, logging, exception handling, restarts, load balancers, and reverse proxy context.
